{
  "domain_type": "alchemical",
  "tokens": ["Zosimos", "divine fire", "matter"],
  "boundary": "The hermetic vessel containing the entire descent and ascent cycle",
  "opcodes": {
    "VINIT":  {"element": "prima materia", "justification": "The undifferentiated, void-like initial substance before any alchemical operation"},
    "TANCH":  {"element": "the hermetic vessel", "justification": "The sealed boundary that contains the entire opus and prevents leakage"},
    "AFWD":   {"element": "sublimation", "justification": "The forward, ascending transformation of matter toward spirit"},
    "AREV":   {"element": "descent of divine fire", "justification": "The reverse, descending movement of spirit into matter — the katabasis"},
    "CLINK":  {"element": "solve et coagula sequence", "justification": "The chaining of dissolution and coagulation operations in order"},
    "IMSCRIB": {"element": "the philosopher's stone", "justification": "The self-recognizing, unchanging essence that remains identical through all transformations"},
    "FSPLIT": {"element": "separatio", "justification": "Splits the composite substance into its volatile (spirit) and fixed (body) components"},
    "FFUSE":  {"element": "coniunctio", "justification": "Reunites the separated volatile and fixed components into the original composite substance"},
    "EVALT":  {"element": "rubedo", "justification": "The red stage, signifying successful completion and perfection"},
    "EVALF":  {"element": "nigredo", "justification": "The black stage, signifying putrefaction and failure of the operation"},
    "ENGAGR": {"element": "albedo", "justification": "The white stage, a paradoxical state where both putrefaction and perfection coexist in potential"},
    "IFIX":   {"element": "the Emerald Tablet", "justification": "The permanent, irreversible inscription of the alchemical process and its laws"}
  },
  "frobenius": {
    "split_element": "separatio",
    "split_input":   "composite substance (body + spirit united)",
    "split_outputs": ["volatile spirit", "fixed body"],
    "fuse_element":  "coniunctio",
    "fuse_result":   "composite substance (body + spirit united)",
    "verdict":       "PASS",
    "failure_reason": ""
  },
  "registers": {
    "void":  "prima materia — unformed, chaotic potential before any operation",
    "true":  "rubedo — the successful, perfected state",
    "false": "nigredo — the failed, putrefied state",
    "both":  "albedo — the paradoxical state where success and failure are simultaneously present"
  },
  "bootstrap": [
    "Step 1 IMSCRIB: The alchemist recognizes the prima materia as the starting point (self-recognition)",
    "Step 2 AREV:   The divine fire descends into matter (katabasis)",
    "Step 3 FSPLIT: The substance is separated into volatile spirit and fixed body (separatio)",
    "Step 4 AFWD:   The volatile spirit ascends, purifying (sublimatio)",
    "Step 5 FFUSE:  The purified spirit reunites with the fixed body (coniunctio)",
    "Step 6 CLINK:  The sequence of solve et coagula is chained repeatedly",
    "Step 7 IFIX:   The final stone is fixed and recorded permanently (the Emerald Tablet)",
    "Step 8 IMSCRIB: The philosopher's stone recognizes itself as the completed work (closure)"
  ],
  "exos": {
    "compiler":  "the alchemist's intention and ritual procedure",
    "ipc":       "sympathetic resonance between spirit and matter within the vessel",
    "memory":    "the alchemical symbols and stages inscribed on the vessel or in the text",
    "scheduler": "the astrological timing and duration of heating/cooling cycles",
    "alfs":      "the Emerald Tablet as the canonical bootstrap store of alchemical principles"
  },
  "entropy": {
    "cost":    "heat and time from the alchemical furnace",
    "pre":     "composite substance in a mixed, unrefined state",
    "post":    "composite substance in a purified, perfected state",
    "verdict": "ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle is conservative because the same substance is merely transformed, not created or destroyed"
  }
}

{

    "TANCH":  {"element": "the practitioner's will", "justification": "the will is the terminal boundary that contains and defines the system"},
    "AFWD":   {"element": "intentional belief", "justification": "forward morphism: adopting a belief temporarily to achieve a goal"},
    "AREV":   {"element": "discarding belief", "justification": "reverse/descent: letting go of the adopted belief after use"},

    "FSPLIT": {"element": "paradigm shift", "justification": "splits the unified will into two distinct belief systems (e.g. 'I believe X' and 'I believe not-X')"},
    "FFUSE":  {"element": "return to core will", "justification": "reconstitutes the original unified will from the two belief branches, recovering the initial state"},
    "EVALT":  {"element": "successful result", "justification": "affirmative: the magickal working achieves its intended effect"},
    "EVALF":  {"element": "failed result", "justification": "negative: the working does not manifest as desired"},
    "ENGAGR": {"element": "paradoxical belief", "justification": "both: holding two contradictory beliefs simultaneously as a technique (e.g. 'this works and does not work')"},
    "IFIX":   {"element": "record in the magickal diary", "justification": "permanent fixation: writing down the method and result, never to be altered"}
  },
  "frobenius": {

    "both":  "11 — the working both succeeds and fails, paradox held"
  },
  "bootstrap": [
    "Step 1 IMSCRIB: the practitioner recognizes themselves as the source of magick",
    "Step 2 AREV:   the practitioner discards all inherited traditions",
    "Step 3 FSPLIT: the practitioner splits their will into two contradictory belief systems",
    "Step 4 AFWD:   the practitioner intentionally adopts one belief system and performs a working",
    "Step 5 FFUSE:  the practitioner returns to the unified will, discarding the adopted belief",
    "Step 6 CLINK:  the practitioner composes a sequence of such paradigm shifts into a ritual",

  "exos": {

  },

{
  "domain_type": "computational",
  "tokens": ["cyber", "mancy", "daemon"],
  "boundary": "the network perimeter that contains all digital processes",
  "opcodes": {
    "VINIT":  {"element": "null pointer", "justification": "void/uninitialized state before any digital object exists"},
    "TANCH":  {"element": "root certificate", "justification": "terminal anchor that closes and authenticates the system"},
    "AFWD":   {"element": "function call", "justification": "forward morphism from caller to callee, transforming input to output"},
    "AREV":   {"element": "return statement", "justification": "reverse/descent from callee back to caller, returning control"},
    "CLINK":  {"element": "pipe operator", "justification": "composition chaining output of one process to input of another"},
    "IMSCRIB": {"element": "digital signature", "justification": "self-recognition: a process signs its own identity"},
    "FSPLIT": {"element": "fork system call", "justification": "splits a process into parent and child branches"},
    "FFUSE":  {"element": "wait system call", "justification": "reconstitutes parent and child into original process state"},
    "EVALT":  {"element": "success exit code (0)", "justification": "affirmative evaluation of a process completion"},
    "EVALF":  {"element": "non-zero exit code", "justification": "negative evaluation indicating error or failure"},
    "ENGAGR": {"element": "race condition", "justification": "both success and failure simultaneously possible in concurrent access"},
    "IFIX":   {"element": "write-once memory (EEPROM)", "justification": "permanent irreversible record of a digital event"}
  },
  "frobenius": {
    "split_element": "fork system call",
    "split_input":   "a running process P",
    "split_outputs": ["parent process P_parent", "child process P_child"],
    "fuse_element":  "wait system call",

    "verdict":       "PASS",
    "failure_reason": ""
  },
  "registers": {
    "void":  "00 — uninitialized memory, no process exists",
    "true":  "01 — process exited with status 0 (success)",
    "false": "10 — process exited with non-zero status (failure)",
    "both":  "11 — both success and failure simultaneously (e.g., partial failure in distributed system)"
  },
  "bootstrap": [
    "Step 1 IMSCRIB: the system recognizes its own digital identity via a self-signed certificate",
    "Step 2 AREV:   the system descends into a lower-level kernel mode to initialize hardware",
    "Step 3 FSPLIT: the init process forks into user-space and kernel-space branches",
    "Step 4 AFWD:   the user-space branch ascends to launch the first user daemon",
    "Step 5 FFUSE:  the kernel waits for the daemon to initialize, then fuses control back",
    "Step 6 CLINK:  the daemon pipes its output to the next service in the boot chain",
    "Step 7 IFIX:   the boot sequence is recorded permanently in the system log",
    "Step 8 IMSCRIB: the system re-asserts its identity after boot is complete"
  ],
  "exos": {
    "compiler":  "the kernel's system call interface that translates user requests into operations",
    "ipc":       "sockets and shared memory for inter-process communication",
    "memory":    "RAM and page tables for state storage and retrieval",
    "scheduler": "the OS scheduler that determines process execution order",
    "alfs":      "the boot ROM containing the initial bootstrap code"
  },
  "entropy": {
    "cost":    "CPU cycles and energy per operation",
    "pre":     "system state before a process cycle (registers, memory)",
    "post":    "system state after the cycle (updated registers, memory)",
    "verdict": "ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle is deterministic and reversible at the instruction level, conserving entropy"
  }
}

{
  "domain_type": "network",
  "tokens": ["worm", "replicate", "spread"],
  "boundary": "the internet itself as a closed network of connected nodes",
  "opcodes": {
    "VINIT":  {"element": "uninitialized payload", "justification": "the worm code before it is executed, a dormant string of bytes"},
    "TANCH":  {"element": "internet boundary", "justification": "the entire internet as the maximal closed system that contains all spread"},
    "AFWD":   {"element": "infection forward", "justification": "the worm propagates from one host to a target via a network connection"},
    "AREV":   {"element": "reverse infection", "justification": "the worm returns to a previously infected host for re-propagation or update"},
    "CLINK":  {"element": "infection chain", "justification": "sequential infection: host A infects host B, then host B infects host C"},
    "IMSCRIB": {"element": "self-identity", "justification": "the worm recognizes itself as the same code across all copies"},
    "FSPLIT": {"element": "replication fork", "justification": "the worm splits into two identical copies sent to two different target hosts"},
    "FFUSE":  {"element": "replication merge", "justification": "two copies of the worm recombine into the original single worm instance on a host"},
    "EVALT":  {"element": "successful infection", "justification": "the worm has successfully installed and executed on a target host"},
    "EVALF":  {"element": "failed infection", "justification": "the worm is blocked by a firewall, antivirus, or network error"},
    "ENGAGR": {"element": "both infection and detection", "justification": "the worm is simultaneously spreading and being detected/analyzed by security systems"},
    "IFIX":   {"element": "persistent payload", "justification": "the worm writes itself to disk or installs a permanent backdoor, irreversible"}
  },
  "frobenius": {
    "split_element": "replication fork",
    "split_input":   "single worm instance on host A",
    "split_outputs": ["worm copy on host B", "worm copy on host C"],
    "fuse_element":  "replication merge",
    "fuse_result":   "single worm instance on host A (recombined from copies B and C)",
    "verdict":       "PASS",
    "failure_reason": ""
  },
  "registers": {
    "void":  "00: dormant worm code in memory, not yet executed",
    "true":  "01: worm has successfully infected and is active",
    "false": "10: worm failed to infect, blocked or inert",
    "both":  "11: worm is both active and being analyzed/blocked simultaneously"
  },
  "bootstrap": [
    "Step 1 IMSCRIB: worm identifies itself as the same code across all copies",
    "Step 2 AREV:   worm returns to a previously infected host to check status",
    "Step 3 FSPLIT: worm replicates to two new target hosts simultaneously",
    "Step 4 AFWD:   worm propagates forward to a new target host",
    "Step 5 FFUSE:  two worm copies merge back into one instance on a host",
    "Step 6 CLINK:  worm chains infections sequentially through hosts",
    "Step 7 IFIX:   worm writes itself permanently to disk on a host",
    "Step 8 IMSCRIB: worm recognizes its persistent identity after fixation"
  ],
  "exos": {
    "compiler":  "worm code generator that assembles the payload for each target OS",
    "ipc":       "network sockets and HTTP/HTTPS protocols for communication between worm instances",
    "memory":    "worm state stored in RAM and on disk as executable files",

  },
  "entropy": {

    "pre":     "worm exists on one host, network resources unutilized",
    "post":    "worm exists on multiple hosts, network resources consumed",

  }
}

{

    "TANCH": {"element": "the host machine's execution sandbox", "justification": "terminal boundary that contains and limits the exploit's effects"},
    "AFWD": {"element": "infection vector (e.g., phishing link)", "justification": "forward morphism delivering payload to target"},
    "AREV": {"element": "reverse shell or back-connection", "justification": "descent morphism enabling control from attacker"},
    "CLINK": {"element": "chained exploit stages (dropper → loader → payload)", "justification": "composition of sequential morphisms to achieve full compromise"},
    "IMSCRIB": {"element": "the ob3ect's self-identity (its code signature)", "justification": "self-recognition that the payload is itself, not a decoy"},
    "FSPLIT": {"element": "replication branching", "justification": "splits into two copies: one to persist locally, one to propagate to new host"},
    "FFUSE": {"element": "reconstitution of original payload from the two copies", "justification": "fusing the local and propagated copies recovers the original exploit code"},
    "EVALT": {"element": "successful infection and propagation", "justification": "affirmative state: payload executed and spread"},
    "EVALF": {"element": "detection and quarantine by antivirus", "justification": "negative state: payload blocked or removed"},
    "ENGAGR": {"element": "payload that both infects and is detected simultaneously", "justification": "paradox: code runs but is flagged, yet still spreads before removal"},
    "IFIX": {"element": "persistent backdoor in host registry", "justification": "irreversible fixation: the backdoor remains even after reboot"}
  },

    "both": "11: payload runs but is also flagged by security software"
  },
  "bootstrap": [
    "Step 1 IMSCRIB: the exploit recognizes its own code signature",
    "Step 2 AREV: payload descends into user memory via buffer overflow",
    "Step 3 FSPLIT: payload splits into local persistence and propagation branches",
    "Step 4 AFWD: propagation branch ascends to new host via network",
    "Step 5 FFUSE: local and propagation copies merge to reconstitute original payload",
    "Step 6 CLINK: chained exploit stages compose to achieve full control",

}

{
  "domain_type": "cognitive",
  "tokens": ["meme", "replicate", "spread"],
  "boundary": "the human cognitive architecture that processes and transmits the meme",
  "opcodes": {
    "VINIT":  {"element": "unencoded potential meme", "justification": "void/uninitialized state before any meme exists"},
    "TANCH":  {"element": "the host mind's belief system", "justification": "terminal anchor that contains and limits meme propagation"},
    "AFWD":   {"element": "encoding into neural patterns", "justification": "forward morphism from abstract meme to neural representation"},
    "AREV":   {"element": "decoding from neural patterns to behavior", "justification": "reverse/descent from internal representation to external action"},
    "CLINK":  {"element": "sequential transmission chain", "justification": "composition of encoding, expression, and reception steps"},
    "IMSCRIB": {"element": "self-recognition of the meme as itself", "justification": "identity: the meme recognizes its own pattern across hosts"},
    "FSPLIT": {"element": "meme replication via branching", "justification": "splits into two copies: one in original host, one in new host"},
    "FFUSE":  {"element": "reconstitution of the meme from its copies", "justification": "fuses the two copies back into the original meme pattern"},
    "EVALT":  {"element": "successful replication and spread", "justification": "affirmative: meme propagates to new host"},
    "EVALF":  {"element": "failed replication or extinction", "justification": "negative: meme fails to transmit or is forgotten"},
    "ENGAGR": {"element": "meme that is both believed and doubted", "justification": "paradox: host simultaneously accepts and questions the meme"},
    "IFIX":   {"element": "engram in long-term memory", "justification": "permanent irreversible record of the meme in neural structure"}
  },
  "frobenius": {
    "split_element": "meme replication via branching",
    "split_input": "the original meme pattern in host A",
    "split_outputs": ["meme pattern in host A", "meme pattern in host B"],
    "fuse_element": "reconstitution of the meme from its copies",
    "fuse_result": "the original meme pattern (identical to split_input)",
    "verdict": "PASS",
    "failure_reason": ""
  },
  "registers": {
    "void":  "no meme present in cognitive space",
    "true":  "meme is actively believed and transmitted",
    "false": "meme is rejected or forgotten",
    "both":  "meme is simultaneously believed and doubted (cognitive dissonance)"
  },
  "bootstrap": [
    "Step 1 IMSCRIB: the meme recognizes its own pattern as a replicable unit",
    "Step 2 AREV:   the meme descends into neural encoding",
    "Step 3 FSPLIT: the meme branches into two copies via transmission",
    "Step 4 AFWD:   each copy ascends to expression in a new host",
    "Step 5 FFUSE:  the copies are recognized as the same meme pattern",
    "Step 6 CLINK:  the transmission chain is composed into a lineage",
    "Step 7 IFIX:   the meme is recorded in long-term memory of both hosts",
    "Step 8 IMSCRIB: the meme closes by recognizing itself as the origin"
  ],
  "exos": {
    "compiler":  "human cognitive faculties (perception, memory, reasoning)",
    "ipc":       "language, gesture, writing, digital media",
    "memory":    "neural engrams and synaptic connections",
    "scheduler": "attention and salience mechanisms",
    "alfs":      "the first meme ever transmitted (protomeme)"
  },
  "entropy": {
    "cost":    "neural energy and attention",
    "pre":     "meme exists in one host",
    "post":    "meme exists in two hosts (or more)",
    "verdict": "ΔS ≈ 0 — replication conserves the meme's informational pattern"
  }
}
